---
title: "Readings"
---

# Course Readings

This page lists all readings for the course, organized by day. Most readings link to their source or a search; **[Find it]** links resolve the exact title. Readings marked **[Provided]** will be made available through the course materials. All others are available online at the links provided.

---

## Pre-Course Readings (Suggested) {#pre-course}

These are optional but recommended to get the most out of the course:

1. **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI.* Portfolio/Penguin. **Chapters 1--2.** [Provided]
   - An accessible introduction to how AI is changing work and why public health professionals need to engage with it.

2. **Topol, E. (2019).** High-performance medicine: the convergence of human and artificial intelligence. *Nature Medicine, 25*(1), 44--56. [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41591-018-0300-7)
   - A landmark review of AI applications in medicine and health, providing context for the course.

3. **World Health Organization. (2021).** *Ethics and governance of artificial intelligence for health.* WHO. [Available online](https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240029200)
   - Read the executive summary (pp. 1--12) for an international perspective on AI ethics in health.

---

## Day 1 Readings {#day-1-readings}

### Required

1. **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI.* Chapters 1--2. [Provided]

2. **Anthropic. (2024).** *Prompt Engineering Guide.* [Available online](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/overview)
   - Practical guide to writing effective prompts. Focus on the "Be clear and direct" and "Use examples" sections.

### Recommended

3. **Vaswani, A., et al. (2017).** Attention is all you need. *Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 30.* [Available online](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762)
   - The original transformer paper. Read for historical context; the technical details are not required for this course.

4. **Governor Shapiro. (2024).** Executive Order: Establishing governance and guardrails for Commonwealth use of artificial intelligence. [Available online](https://www.governor.pa.gov/newsroom/governor-shapiro-signs-executive-order-establishing-governance-guardrails-for-commonwealth-use-of-artificial-intelligence/)
   - Pennsylvania's approach to AI governance in state agencies.

---

## Day 2 Readings {#day-2-readings}

### Required

1. **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence.* Chapter 3: "AI as a Creative." [Provided]

2. **Jowsey, T., Corral Barrios, G., & Taggart, A. (2024).** AI-assisted thematic analysis: the future of qualitative data analysis in health professions education research? *Medical Education, 58*(11), 1276--1278. [Find it](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=Jowsey%20AI-assisted%20thematic%20analysis%20future%20qualitative%20data%20analysis%20health%20professions%20education)
   - Critical examination of using AI for qualitative data analysis. Key reading for Activity 4.

### Recommended

3. **OpenDataPhilly. (2024).** Heat Vulnerability Index --- Metadata and Documentation. [Available online](https://opendataphilly.org/datasets/heat-vulnerability-index/)
   - Review the dataset documentation before class to prepare for Activity 3.

4. **Meskó, B., & Topol, E. (2023).** The imperative for regulatory oversight of large language models (or generative AI) in healthcare. *npj Digital Medicine, 6*(1), 120. [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41746-023-00873-0)

5. **Gichoya, J. W., et al. (2022).** AI recognition of patient race in medical imaging: a modelling study. *The Lancet Digital Health, 4*(6), e406--e414. [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1016/S2589-7500(22)00063-2)
   - Demonstrates how AI can identify race from medical images in ways humans cannot, raising concerns about automated bias.

---

## Day 3 Readings {#day-3-readings}

### Required

1. **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence.* Chapter 4: "AI as a Coworker." [Provided]

2. **Anthropic. (2025).** *Claude Code Documentation.* [Available online](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code)
   - Overview of the Claude Code CLI tool. Skim the getting started section and capabilities overview.

### Recommended

3. **Microsoft Research. (2023).** AutoGen: Enabling next-gen LLM applications via multi-agent conversation. [Available online](https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.08155)
   - Technical paper on multi-agent AI systems. Read the introduction and discussion sections for context.

4. **Google. (2025).** *Google AI Studio Documentation.* [Available online](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/ai-studio-quickstart)
   - Getting started guide for Google AI Studio. Review before Activity 4.

5. **Ollama. (2025).** *Ollama Documentation.* [Available online](https://ollama.com/)
   - Review if you plan to install Ollama for Activity 6.

---

## Day 4 Readings {#day-4-readings}

### Required

1. **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence.* Chapters 5--6: "AI as an Expert" and "AI as a Coach." [Provided]

2. **Mollick, E., & Mollick, L. (2024).** Using AI to implement effective teaching strategies in classrooms: Five strategies, including prompts. *The Wharton School Research Paper.* [Available online](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4391243)
   - While focused on education, the frameworks for AI-human collaboration are directly applicable to public health workflow design.

### Recommended

3. **Philadelphia Department of Public Health. (2024).** *Community Health Assessment.* [Available online](https://www.phila.gov/programs/community-health-assessment/)
   - Background reading for the scenario-based activities. Review the executive summary and key findings.

4. **Lee, E. C., et al. (2023).** Artificial intelligence for public health surveillance: opportunities and challenges. *Annual Review of Public Health, 44*, 349--373. [Find it](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=Lee%20Artificial%20intelligence%20for%20public%20health%20surveillance%20opportunities%20and%20challenges%20Annual%20Review%20of%20Public%20Health%202023)

---

## Day 5 Readings {#day-5-readings}

### Required

1. **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence.* Chapters 7--8. [Provided]

2. **Obermeyer, Z., Powers, B., Vogeli, C., & Mullainathan, S. (2019).** Dissecting racial bias in an algorithm used to manage the health of populations. *Science, 366*(6464), 447--453. [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342)
   - Essential reading on algorithmic bias in healthcare. This is the real case discussed in Activity 1, Dilemma 4.

### Recommended

3. **Mitchell, M., et al. (2019).** Model cards for model reporting. *Proceedings of the Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency*, 220--229. [Available online](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.03993)
   - The original model cards paper. Key reading on AI transparency.

4. **Gebru, T., et al. (2021).** Datasheets for datasets. *Communications of the ACM, 64*(12), 86--92. [Available online](https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09010)
   - Companion to the model cards paper, focused on documenting training data.

5. **Benjamin, R. (2019).** *Race After Technology: Abolitionist Tools for the New Jim Code.* Polity Press. [Publisher](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=Race%20After%20Technology%20Ruha%20Benjamin)
   - Chapter 1: "Engineered Inequity." [Provided excerpt]
   - Critical examination of how technology can reinforce racial inequality.

6. **Vayena, E., Blasimme, A., & Cohen, I. G. (2018).** Machine learning in medicine: addressing ethical challenges. *PLoS Medicine, 15*(11), e1002689. [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pmed.1002689)

7. **Char, D. S., Shah, N. H., & Magnus, D. (2018).** Implementing machine learning in health care---addressing ethical challenges. *New England Journal of Medicine, 378*(11), 981--983. [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1056/NEJMp1714229)

---

## Additional Qualitative Research Readings {#qualitative-readings}

For participants interested in AI-assisted qualitative research:

1. **Jowsey, T., Corral Barrios, G., & Taggart, A. (2024).** AI-assisted thematic analysis: the future of qualitative data analysis in health professions education research? *Medical Education, 58*(11), 1276--1278. [Find it](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=Jowsey%20AI-assisted%20thematic%20analysis%20future%20qualitative%20data%20analysis%20health%20professions%20education) (Also listed under Day 2.)

2. **Dai, S. C., Stedman, A., Cheng, C. K., Chauhan, S., Liang, R., & Leung, F. H. (2024).** Using large language models to generate qualitative research interview guides on patient experiences. *JMIR Medical Education, 10*, e59608. [Available online](https://doi.org/10.2196/59608)

3. **Marathe, S., & Toyama, K. (2018).** Semi-automated coding for qualitative research: A user-centered inquiry and initial prototypes. *Proceedings of the 2018 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems*, 1--12. [Find it](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=Marathe%20Toyama%20Semi-automated%20coding%20for%20qualitative%20research%20user-centered%20inquiry%20CHI%202018)

4. **Tai, R. (2024).** An assessment of the quality of qualitative research conducted using AI tools like ChatGPT. *Preprint.* [Find it](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=Tai%20assessment%20quality%20qualitative%20research%20AI%20tools%20ChatGPT%202024)

---

## Recommended Resources {#recommended-resources}

### Books

- **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI.* Portfolio/Penguin.
  - The primary course text. Accessible, practical, and grounded in research.

- **Benjamin, R. (2019).** *Race After Technology: Abolitionist Tools for the New Jim Code.* Polity Press.
  - Essential reading on technology, race, and justice.

- **O'Neil, C. (2016).** *Weapons of Math Destruction: How Big Data Increases Inequality and Threatens Democracy.* Crown.
  - Accessible introduction to algorithmic bias, with public health-relevant examples.

- **Topol, E. (2019).** *Deep Medicine: How Artificial Intelligence Can Make Healthcare Human Again.* Basic Books.
  - Optimistic but thoughtful perspective on AI in healthcare.

### Substacks and Newsletters

- **Ethan Mollick** --- [One Useful Thing](https://www.oneusefulthing.org/) --- Regular, accessible updates on AI developments with practical implications
- **The Batch** --- [deeplearning.ai](https://www.deeplearning.ai/the-batch/) --- Andrew Ng's weekly AI newsletter
- **Import AI** --- [Jack Clark](https://importai.substack.com/) --- Policy-focused AI newsletter

### Online Courses

- **Google.** [Introduction to Generative AI Learning Path](https://cloud.google.com/learn/training/machinelearning-AI) --- Free, self-paced
- **Stanford HAI.** [Artificial Intelligence in Health Care](https://online.stanford.edu/courses/cs472-data-models-and-learning-health-care) --- More technical
- **Coursera.** [AI for Everyone](https://www.coursera.org/learn/ai-for-everyone) by Andrew Ng --- Non-technical introduction

### Organizations

- **Partnership on AI** --- [partnershiponai.org](https://partnershiponai.org/) --- Multi-stakeholder organization studying AI's impact on people and society
- **AI Now Institute** --- [ainowinstitute.org](https://ainowinstitute.org/) --- Research institute examining social implications of AI
- **Stanford HAI** --- [hai.stanford.edu](https://hai.stanford.edu/) --- Stanford's Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence
